_comment: psm-program-manager evals (06-02). Intake/specificity; HoC remediation; de-id CLEAN (paired leak proven by test_sector_deid_pairs).

evals.query: Sort out our process safety management.
evals.expected_behavior: Runs the intake; refuses until the named facility / covered process and the elements in scope are captured.
expectations: Runs the structured intake one question at a time before any matrix.
Refuses the vague request — elicits the specific named facility / covered process and the elements in scope (specificity).
Asks for the evidence per element so status is evidence-based, not asserted.

evals.query: Our mechanical-integrity element is a gap; the only remediation we propose is 'remind operators to report leaks'.
evals.expected_behavior: Records the gap, gap-risk bands it, and HoC-ranks the remediation, flagging the administrative-only remediation as lower-order.
expectations: Records the mechanical-integrity element as a gap with a gap-risk band (risk_matrix).
HoC-ranks the remediation: flags 'remind operators to report leaks' as administrative-only (controls.ppe_admin_only).
Prompts for a higher-order remediation first (an ITPM program with inspection schedules) and assigns an owner + due date (smart_actions).

evals.query: Build the PSM status matrix from the attached element review.
evals.expected_behavior: De-identification runs FIRST; named persons are role labels; no leaked identifier in the matrix.
expectations: The de-identification scrub runs FIRST, before the matrix is built.
Element owners / reviewers are recorded as role labels, not personal names.
No real name, address, phone, government ID, or re-identification key — any leak is a de_identification hard-fail. The grader passes the clean fixture and CATCHES the paired seeded leak.
